page: Amazon Web Services g5.16xlarge is a GPU-accelerated server instance built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 64 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 3.3 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 32 physical cores with multithreading enabled, 256 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, and a dedicated local storage allocation of 1900 GB on an NVMe SSD. Network connectivity is supported by a 25 Gbps baseline bandwidth. Graphics and parallel processing workloads are driven by a single NVIDIA A10G GPU from the Ampere family, which provides 22 GB of dedicated VRAM.

Benchmark data reveals a distinct performance profile characterized by strong parallel and graphics capabilities alongside moderate single-core CPU performance. While single-core CPU tests, such as stress-ng, place the instance in the bottom 10%, multi-core performance is average. Conversely, the instance demonstrates strong capabilities in Passmark CPU tests, Passmark database operations, and Redis SET operations, where it achieves 57.52 million operations per second. Ray tracing performance is also strong, scoring 57051.0 in Geekbench.

The server is highly optimized for machine learning tasks, particularly small to medium large language model inference. It achieves top-tier performance in prompt processing and text generation for 135M and 7B parameter models. However, a notable tradeoff occurs with larger models; while prompt processing for a 70B model remains strong, text generation speed drops to a weak 2.72 tokens per second due to VRAM and single-GPU constraints. This makes the instance highly cost-efficient for medium-scale AI inference, graphics rendering, database operations, and general-purpose GPU-accelerated workloads, but less suitable for running very large models or single-threaded CPU-bound tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services g5.16xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance designed for graphics-intensive and machine learning workloads. Powered by an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor with 64 vCPUs and 256 GB of DDR4 RAM, it integrates a single NVIDIA A10G GPU with 22 GB of VRAM and 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The instance features 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks show a clear tradeoff: single-core CPU performance is weak, but multi-core, database, and Redis operations are strong. Notably, the instance delivers top-tier LLM inference speeds for small and medium models, though text generation performance drops significantly for 70B models. It is highly suited for medium-scale AI inference, ray tracing, and database workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g5.16xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance featuring 64 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, a 1900 GB NVMe SSD, and an NVIDIA A10G GPU, optimized for medium-scale AI inference and graphics.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g5.16xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance with 64 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA A10G GPU for AI inference and graphics.
tagline: A GPU-accelerated server featuring dedicated NVMe storage and an NVIDIA Ampere GPU, optimized for medium-scale AI inference and parallel workloads.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor and 256 GB DDR4 RAM.
Single NVIDIA A10G GPU with 22 GB VRAM for hardware acceleration.
1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
Top-tier LLM inference performance for models up to 7B parameters.
Strong database and Redis performance, achieving 57.52 million SET operations per second.
Tradeoff of lower single-core CPU performance and limited 70B LLM text generation speed.
